name: Kon
description: Kon, in Incan mythology, was a creator god associated with the coast of Peru. He was often depicted as a winged figure, reflecting his ability to fly and move swiftly. Before Viracocha, Kon was believed to have created the first race of humans, populating the coastal regions of Peru. These early humans were said to have been simple and easily pleased. However, Kon, in his generosity or perhaps impatience, provided them with easy access to food and resources, leading to laziness and complacency. Annoyed by their lack of appreciation and industry, Kon punished them by transforming the fertile land into a barren desert. He also took away their ability to easily obtain food, forcing them to work harder for their sustenance. Kon is often considered a trickster figure, as his actions, while sometimes seemingly benevolent, often resulted in hardship and suffering. His departure from the Incan pantheon coincided with the arrival of Viracocha, who is credited with reshaping the world and creating a new race of humans. While not as widely revered as other Incan deities, Kon's story serves as a cautionary tale about the importance of hard work and gratitude. His influence is evident in the arid landscape of the Peruvian coast and the challenges faced by its inhabitants. Kon's powers included creation, transformation, and control over the land and its resources. He embodies the unpredictable nature of the world and the consequences of human actions.
subtitle: Coastal Creator and Punisher God

keyMyths.title: Kon Creates the First Humans
keyMyths.description: Kon created the first race of humans and gave them easy access to food. However, he punished them for their laziness.

keyMyths.title: Kon Transforms the Land
keyMyths.description: Annoyed by the first humans, Kon transformed the fertile land into a desert. This forced them to work hard for survival.

keyMyths.title: Kon's Departure
keyMyths.description: Kon's influence waned with the arrival of Viracocha. Viracocha reshaped the world and created a new human race.

sources.author: Maria Rostworowski de Diez Canseco
